author: @rickparker8036
text: Thank you for the tour. Another fantastic video. You can’t mess and I still don’t know why you don’t have millions of followers. Keep it up!

author: @NorthernKitty
text:
You live a very blessed life, Rob!!   Thanks for sharing part of it with us, it's fantastic to see the setting in which you live!! ☺️  I always wanted to visit both England and Ireland, and... who knows?  Maybe someday I'll actually follow through on that whim.  If I do, I'll be sure to stop by your shop!!  ☺️☺️☺️

The name Canterbury reminds me of course of The Canterbury Tales by Geoffery Chaucer, which contains one of my favorite quotes:
"It seems to me that poverty is an eyeglass through which one may see his true friends."
That's an eyeglass I stumbled across in my lifetime, and have been very grateful for what it reveals. 😋

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

author: @deslomeslager
text: Hint: use a tablet, keep it upright, in front of you with google street loaded in Canterbury. Now you can look around by rotating yourself (with the tablet), an office chair does this for you. If you do this for a minute or so (walking around virtually), you will actually get the feeling you are there. Maybe in the near future Google will make a 3D version, one which you can see with the goggles on.

author: @AndreaDingbatt
text:
Thank you so much for taking us around your lovely home town, It's so beautiful!! 

I think that mentioning the equine connection, was simply a ruse to grab my attention, Lol!
(I do still miss working with them.)

It has such an amazing history, and really so kind of you to think of us, whilst you are actually on a Pub-Crawl! 
Ah - Ha, you have been sussed!

I live in Carlisle, I've been here about 40years, plenty of travelling & working elsewhere. 

I am often astonished by how sometimes we can take our surroundings for granted, but I guess familiarity can be the problem.

I used to run mothers B&B, and one of the many pluses, was taking guests on free guided tours, 
I enjoy walking, and the history and beauty of Carlisle is still very much in the forefront of my mind. 

Taking people to see the architecture, parks, the Castle and the Cathedral, kept my eyes open for things I'd see every day, so showing people around, was absolutely brilliant!! 

Great to see you appreciating this stunning, historic and progressive town.
Andrea, Jasper and George the Pigeon. XxX.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I have been to Carlisle - it's very red if memory serves lol

replies.author: @AndreaDingbatt
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering Lol, Thank you, and you're right, it is!!😅XxX.
